change_request: A written done-line is FROZEN — it is the contract you set before the work, and no session edits it in place (§9.4; the log's own rule: never edit a line, only append). Changing what "done" meant is not a typo fix — it is abandoning a bar you committed to, a moment of shame, and the path is deliberately loud and painful: python -m loop.pen supersede-done --abandoning <id> --slug <new-slug> --reason "<an honest reflection on why the bar you set could not be met — glib excuses are refused>" --by <who>. It writes a NEW done-line (the original stands forever as history), records a loud done_superseded admission, and is NEVER self-authorized: a session's supersede sits pending until bdo stamps it (no one signs their own line). Complete tasks and mark them done; do not move the line.
scaffold: Written before code, per §9.4. When this line is met, stop.
> **Done when:** <the one line that, when true, means stop>
